Paper Title: Spatial congruence between biodiversity and ecosystem services in South Africa
Study Population / Unit of Analysis: Grid squares spanning the semi-arid landscape of South Africa. The resolution is quarter-degree squares (QDS), approximately 700 km².
Independent Variable Unit: Surface water supply (not reported | runoff); Water flow regulation (not reported | percentage contribution of groundwater to base flows); Carbon storage (not reported | carbon storage potentials for different vegetation types); Soil accumulation (not reported | function of soil depth and leaf litter accumulation index); Soil retention (not reported | function of vegetation cover and erodibility)
Dependent Variable Unit: Birds (the richest 10% of grid cells | number of species); Frogs (the richest 10% of grid cells | number of species); Butterfly (the richest 10% of grid cells | number of species); Mammals (the richest 10% of grid cells | number of species); Animals combined (the richest 10% of grid cells | number of species); Threatened and endemic plants (the richest 10% of grid cells | number of species); Threatened plants (the richest 10% of grid cells | number of species); Endemic plants (the richest 10% of grid cells | number of species); Vegetation diversity (1 to 17 | number of vegetation types per QDS)

Paper Title: Ecosystem services from a degraded peatland of Central Kalimantan: implications for policy, planning, and management
